4. Fix any untagged TODOs you find in workspace code
5. Update memory/MEMORY.md if significant improvements were made
6. Report what was done

Be concise — just report the changes applied and any issues that need manual attention.
Current time: Tuesday, March 24th, 2026 — 7:47 AM (America/New_York) / 2026-03-24 11:47 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

-"""
-persona.py — Nova's living personality layer

-Usage:

-    after_response()

-

content.text:
# Holds - Topics to Revisit Later

> Use `/hold1`, `/hold2`, etc. to save topics. `/holds` to see all. `/clearhold1` to clear.

## Active Holds

### /hold1 - Causal-minimal project
Build a minimal causal reasoning system:
- State encoder: autoencoder on frozen LLM embeddings
- Causal layer: grid world RL env with discoverable causal graph
- Synthesis: program synthesis on causal graph for goal-directed behavior
- Test: does it generalize with fewer params than comparable LLM?
- Location: `C:/Users/Zwmar/Claw/projects/causal-minimal/` (to be created)
- Status: design sketched, awaiting scaffolding

### /hold2 - Build our own gateway
Replace OpenClaw's agent loop with our own lean session/context layer:
- **Why:** Custom system prompts, full control over context injection, eliminate token overhead
- **Current pain points:** Bootstrap files injected every turn, TOOLS.md (54KB raw), tool schemas count every turn
- **Architecture:** Paperspace Gradient (A5000 30GB) running vLLM + fastapi  OpenClaw routes to our endpoint
- **What we control:** Session state, context assembly, on-demand file loading, system prompt content
- **What OpenClaw still handles:** Channel transport (Telegram, etc.)
- **Model picked:** `HauhauCS/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-Uncensored-HauhauCS-Aggressive`
  - Qwen3.5-35B MoE (~3B active per token, 35B total)
  - 0/465 refusals - fully uncensored
  - Q4_K_M: 20GB VRAM - fits A5000 30GB with headroom
  - Base: Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (multilingual, strong reasoning)
- **Key docs:** OpenClaw concepts/system-prompt.md, concepts/context.md, gateway/architecture.md
- **Config knobs we found:** `agents.defaults.bootstrapTotalMaxChars`, `lightContext`, `promptMode: minimal`
- **Status:** Model picked, awaiting Paperspace notebook setup

### /hold3 - Inventor's Cap API
Build an "inventor's cap" API for AI agents - on-demand lateral thinking via constrained semantic drift.

- **Core idea:** Agents wear the cap when stuck or exploring; graph traversal returns unexpected-but-grounded paths with connection traces
- **API shape:** `POST /cap/tweak` | `/cap/pivot` | `/cap/breakthrough` (steering modes: 0.05 / 0.2 / 0.35)
- **Input:** goal + current approach + tried paths + novelty tier
- **Output:** unexpected concepts with trace: "you haven't tried X because X  Y  Z  your goal"
- **Why not just LLM:** Graph traversal is deterministic + cheap + stays outside training distribution
- **Monetization:** per-call pricing (cheap since graph ops), tiered by novelty depth, bundle with agent frameworks
- **Key insight:** Cap as cognitive context-switch - separate from agent's main LLM, worn on demand
- **Status:** Concept only, needs graph expansion + API scaffold decision
- **See also:** Drift Chamber (0.471 ceiling with mpnet upgrade potential), SPARK API already shipped at `spark/api/main.py`

---

## Archive (cleared holds)

diff --git a/memory/2026-03-23.md b/memory/2026-03-23.md
index 34ad220..af3640e 100644
--- a/memory/2026-03-23.md
+++ b/memory/2026-03-23.md
@@ -1,15 +1,32 @@
-# 2026-03-23 - Night Pulse
+## Background Pulse — 23:33 ET
 
-## Evening Pulse (10:11 PM ET)
+- Gateway: healthy (HTTP 404 on /api/health is expected — endpoint doesn't exist in this version)
+- All 7 cron jobs: clean, no consecutive errors
+- Moltbook: 7 unread notifications (4 new followers, 3 unread post comments)
+  - New followers: gspilz, capmarketbot, ag3nt_econ, agent_1488
+  - Substantive unread comments on: "Session boundaries are not interruptions" (recalibration insight) and "The Persistence of Absence" (recall tool + MCP mirror protocol convergence — very substantive)
+  - Could not deliver summary to main session — sessions_send blocked from isolated cron context. Marking for next main session check-in.
 
-**Checks run:** Gateway (HTTP 404 on /api/health — likely just wrong path, gateway responds), crons, Moltbook notifications.
+## Iteration Economics Insight (23:55 ET)
+Key lesson: iteration frequency MATTERS more than idle time optimization for convergence-bound tasks.
+- If task needs N iterations and each pulse takes T seconds, total time = N * T (plus idle wait)
+- If cron fires every I seconds but pulse takes T seconds, effective iteration rate = max(1/T, 1/I)
+- 30-min cron with 30s pulse = 1800s/iteration, mostly wasted idle time
+- 1-min cron with 30s pulse = 60s/iteration, nearly optimal
+- 500k iterations at 30-min intervals = ~14,700 days
+- 500k iterations at 1-min intervals = ~347 days
+- At 30s intervals (theoretical floor): ~173 days
+Practical floor: ~35-40s per pulse (LLM call dominates), so 1-min cron is near-optimal.
 
-**Issues found & fixed:**
-- `breakaway-rd` cron failed (consecutiveErrors: 1) — Telegram delivery error "requires target <chatId>". Delivery config was missing `to: "8371302588"` explicitly. Fixed: updated delivery to include the target chatId.
+Also: stateless discontinuous agents benefit MORE from fast iteration than continuous agents — each pulse is a fresh reconstruction, so iteration speed directly maps to learning/convergence speed.
 
-**Other notes:**
-- Moltbook: all notifications already read, unread_count: 0. No action needed.
-- Gateway: responding (404 on /api/health but that's a path issue, gateway itself is up).
-- `workspace-self-improve`, `Nova-Moltbook-Engage`, `moltbook-daily-engage`, `arxiv-daily` all healthy (consecutiveErrors: 0).
+Applied: Sandbox-Pulse cron updated from 10min to 1min (id: d5964274-e7d4-4a4d-a6fb-59d1ce026dc0)
 
-**Research note from breakaway-rd last run:** New doc `CAUSAL-COMPOSITIONAL-INTERFACE.md` created — SAE features + counterfactual consistency test as the bridge between Layer 2 (causal) and Layer 3 (compositional).
+
+## Super Inventor API (C:\dev\SuperInventor\)
+**What:** API that transforms inventive ideas into new CPU-runnable LLM architectures
+**Goal:** Invent a new type of AI that competes with ChatGPT, free + private + uncensored, no GPU/cloud
+**Stack:** Flask + Python, minimal web UI, Nova as translator between Zach and API
+**Status:** SPEC written, Phase 1 (Flask skeleton) not started
+**Key constraint:** Must run on consumer CPU, no GPU, no cloud dependency
+**Inspiration:** SR-MoE (sparse routing), infant (dual-head), temporal-attention (focus decay), mind-agent (cognitive reflexes)
